About Me

Accounting roots,
data-driven mind.

I started my career as an accountant because numbers have always felt like a language to me. Five years and thousands of transactions later, I realised the story gets far more interesting when you bring data analytics into the room.

I have improved financial reporting accuracy by 45%, cut invoice errors by 70% through automation, and managed over 2,000 monthly transactions with zero discrepancies. That foundation sits underneath everything I do as a data and business analyst.

I am Community Manager at Clarity Movement, guiding aspiring analysts across Nigeria. I am also a GCUB scholarship applicant pursuing a Master’s degree in Data Science.

Selected Work

Projects & Analysis

⛶ Zoom

Financial Modelling — Cashflow Analysis

Zumi Holdings: 24-Month Cashflow & Runway Model

A 12-sheet cashflow model for a Nigerian multi-brand tech holding company. With ₦142M in the bank, the base case confirms 12+ months of runway — but three customers hold 62% of all outstanding receivables, making concentration risk the critical vulnerability the board hasn’t priced in.

  • Worst case turns cash negative December 2025 due to ₦18.4M CIT instalment coinciding with delayed AR collection
  • Series A raise of ₦400M in Q1 2026 extends closing cash to ₦584M under best case
  • ₦80.78M in committed CapEx across Oct 2025 to Jun 2026 requires sequenced drawdown planning
  • Three debt facilities (GTBank, BOI, Renaissance Capital) totalling ₦137M with ₦5.58M monthly service
ExcelCashflow Forecasting Scenario AnalysisAR AgeingStress Testing
⇩ Download Excel View Repository →
⛶ Zoom

Unit Economics — Break-Even Analysis

Zumi Eats: Unit Economics & Break-Even Model

A per-order profitability model across 5,663 real order records over 90 days in Lagos, Abuja and Port Harcourt. The network needs 115 orders per day to break even but receives only 63 — every city loses money before a single naira of profit is possible.

  • Network average contribution margin of ₦1,594 per order; food cost alone consumes ₦2,484
  • Jumia Food 25% commission, Glovo 22%, Bolt 18%; Direct channel charges zero
  • Lagos needs 60 orders/day (achieves 39); Abuja needs 34 (achieves 15); PH needs 20 (achieves 8)
  • Aggregator rate +3pts with food cost +10% pushes daily break-even from 115 to 155 orders
ExcelUnit Economics Break-EvenSensitivity Analysis5,663 Orders
⇩ Download Excel View Repository →
⛶ Zoom

Ledger Reconciliation — P&L Reporting

Zumi Holdings: Q3 2025 Group Ledger & P&L

1,436 raw transactions across three source systems cleaned and reconciled into an auditable Q3 Profit and Loss report. Zumi Holdings lost ₦98.8M in Q3 2025. All three brands operated at a loss. Six data quality issues totalling ₦6.44M were identified and corrected.

  • Zumi Mart is the largest loss-maker at ₦48.7M; inventory costs exceed revenue by 2.3x
  • Duplicate Distell Wholesale invoice (₦1.84M) and duplicate payroll entry (₦680K) excluded
  • FX conversion applied across three currencies at documented month-end rates
  • Full bank reconciliation across GTBank NGN operating and USD domiciliary accounts
ExcelPower QueryData Cleaning P&L ReportingFX Conversion1,436 Transactions
⇩ Download Excel View Repository →
⛶ Zoom

Sales Analytics — Power BI Dashboard

Superstore Sales Performance Analysis

A two-page interactive Power BI dashboard built on 5,009 orders across 4 years (2014–2017). Revenue of $1.10M, profit of $132.52K analysed across states, segments, categories and ship modes. Discounts above 30% are destroying profit; Tables alone loses $11K annually.

  • Technology drives 53% of profit ($70.2K) despite matching Furniture in revenue; Furniture margin is just 2%
  • Tables sub-category loses $11K annually; Bookcases also loss-making; Supplies marginally negative
  • Zero-discount orders generate $149K profit — discounting strategy destroys $16.7K net
  • Consumer segment is 51% of revenue ($558K); West and East regions generate 60% of all sales
Power BIExcelDAX Sales Analytics5,009 Orders
